description: Sir Nigel was appointed Chair of the Committee on Radioactive Waste Management on 2 July 2018. Until 2017, Sir Nigel was the Executive Director of Schwarzman Scholars. He is one of the world’s leading human geographers and previously served as Vice-Chancellor and President of the University of Warwick and as Pro-Vice-Chancellor for Research at the University of Oxford. Term of office ends: January 2028 (extended)

details.body:
Sir Nigel was appointed Chair of the Committee on Radioactive Waste Management on 2 July 2018.

Until 2017, Sir Nigel was the Executive Director of Schwarzman Scholars. He is one of the world’s leading human geographers and previously served as Vice-Chancellor and President of the University of Warwick and as Pro-Vice-Chancellor for Research at the University of Oxford.

Term of office ends: January 2028 (extended)

Sir Nigel was appointed Chair of the Committee on Radioactive Waste Management on 2 July 2018.

Until 2017, Sir Nigel was the Executive Director of Schwarzman Scholars. He is one of the world’s leading human geographers and previously served as Vice-Chancellor and President of the University of Warwick and as Pro-Vice-Chancellor for Research at the University of Oxford.

Term of office ends: January 2028 (extended)

The Chair ensures the quality and delivery of CoRWM’s work programme and also that all output maintains the committee’s impartiality and authority.

The Chair is responsible for

  • managing the relationship with the departments and ministers that sponsor the Committee
  • making sure that CoRWM’s work programme is aligned with government programmes for radioactive waste management

The Chair is appointed for a 4-year period and is expected to spend about 1 and a half days per week on CoRWM business.

title: Sir Nigel Thrift
